reviews.review_text:
Great game. Has a native linux port. I love that! 

If you are on debian make sure to install steam from the steam website / .deb file. not from the debian apt repo. Otherwise  the game won't launch because of steam path issues. Once I figured that out it runs flawlessly
actually the perfect game for people who do NOT crash out easily, and are super bad at platformers. if you fit that criteria, i believe that this would be the perfect game for you sire......

This is my favourite game of all time. Nothing makes me feel a greater sense of accomplishment more than completing a hard room. 7c and Farewell last room took me so long that my muscle memory for the movements genuinely might be permanently etched in my brain forever. 

Thank you Celeste. Thank you Maddy Makes Games. Thank you Maddy Thorson.
One of the best platformers, very charming  style with good difficulty curve and thoughtful level design
Its not for me, I don't care for slamming my head into a wall 1000 times trying to hit these precision jumps to get through the level. If you don't like precision platformers its not for you

I figure it's time to actually review Celeste after 4-5 years of purchasing and 100%ing it.

Celeste is a challenging precision platformer [i]but you already know that, right?[/i] What makes this game special apart from its apparent difficulty?

To me, it'd be the sense of determination that it awakens through your multitudes of attempts at going through a level / screen. I never felt the game was at fault for me dying, rather it allowed me to see the error in my execution or planning. [i][strike]or others when I am watching them play :P[/strike][/i]

That with the fluid movement feel of the game and its more intricate and wide movement techs just makes you feel *super* when you flash through a level.

But, all that is when you've actually given time and practice, yeah? Not exactly.
Even on your first playthrough, the gradual (sometimes a spike) in difficulty allows you to get comfortable and learn as you progress through. And sooner or later, you'll find yourself flashing through the previous levels.

Reviewing stories isn't something I'm quite good at so all I can say is it is enjoyable to go through, seeing Madeline go and pass through each hurdle that comes across. Music is a bit more subjective as well so cannot give a good review other than I personally like it.

My fingers legit hurt after finishing the toughest levels, yet I don't regret anything. No other platformer I’ve played comes close to the level of input precision and persistence that this game demands.

What I love most is how all the mechanics build on top of each other. I’m always surprised when the game introduces a new use for dashes and jumps that I hadn't even considered. It creates remarkable depth from a simple moveset that anyone can pick up.

From the mechanics to the sound and visuals, as well as the difficulty, everything just feels great. Finding a better 2D platformer is close to impossible. It really shows why so many developers take inspiration from it.

Be careful! If you've downloaded Celeste on itch.io or a different method, and try to play the game through Steam, it will overwrite your first save file! I nearly lost my original save file with around 190 strawberries unlocked, luckily I could restore it with the backups the devs had the insight to set up.

That being said, this is a game worth buying twice. The first time, I purchased Celeste through an itch.io bundle at a steep discount, but I love this game so dearly I bought it again here to officially unlock all the achievements. My real playtime is more than 300 hours. Snappy, satisfying gameplay, gorgeous pixel art, a thriving mod community, I'm sure it's all been said at this point. Fantastic game. But BE CAREFUL with those save files.

My genuine favorite video game of all time  (no spoilers in this review)

At the time of writing this I have every steam achievement and working on 100%ing it (maybe one day). It is really hard, but it doesn't feel annoying because most of the rooms are only about 20 seconds long meaning that if (when) you die you don't lose much time. Additionally, respawn time is short meaning you can just keep trying until you get it right. Each level comes with it's own mechanics and feel so it never feels like it's repeating the same screen. There are some spots where it's possible to get lost, but if you just explore a little bit, it's not too confusing, and it's mainly only one specific section of one of the levels. Then, If you finish the story there are many things that use the mechanics of the levels, but make them harder for more dedicated players. The story is pretty good, but I don't really have much to say about it other than that with this being a spoiler-free review. 

The difficulty curve is amazing. (so amazing I had to give it it's own paragraph.) You will not notice the levels getting harder. You will not notice you getting better. They fine tuned it so well. However, there are those few glorious screens where the difficulty drops. Not enough for you to be able to see it before you go through. Occasionally though, it's just enough to let you beat the screen on your first try. Then you look back on it and say, 
"well, that wasn't so hard". You see it, though, and you recognize how far you've come. You recognize that you wouldn't even begin to be able to come at this only a bit before. From that point on, this game has you in it's clutches, and you will never complain about repeatedly bashing your head into a brick wall for ten hours straight again. 

All in all, if you buy this game, expect to die a lot. After that, expect to be happy because the camera moved. I'm talking like you won't make any progress in the first hour, but I already said the difficulty curve is good: it starts out fairly easy, relax. Also, I said "you will" a lot in this review. It should be stated that all this comes solely from my own experience, but presumably we are both humans, so it's not the worst indicator for what your experience might be like. After all, it's good enough to make some crazy lunatic write a three paragraph essay on why you should buy it. (not a very well structured one though)

it took me TOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO long to get all the achievos, but in the year of our horse 2026 i have finally done it MEANING i can finally leave a review.
...well ok. it's celeste. everybody has heard of celeste and likely only heard good things about it. i probably don't have to preach it very much. i may not be very good at the game but it's satisfying to grind out a task and then eventually complete it, even if the game spares no stops for silly casual players. the modding environment is also great and it's very accessible which is awesome!

lesbians (if you're brave enough...)

step 2 start new save file
step 3 play tutorial
step 4 get to chapter 3
step 5 die
step 6 die
step 7 rage quit
step 8 beat the game
step 9 pure happyness

The best platformer I've played in my life. It gives you a simple moveset (jump and a dash), and keeps building upon it for the entirety of it's course. Celeste's story is unexpectedly good for a game like this, I was expecting random tidbits here and there to explain Madeline's motives, but I was pleasantly surprised by it's worldbuilding. It's also cheap, has a banging soundtrack, what else do you need?

Best game ever. Down to the platforming, story, and gameplay, I must say it is my favorite game of all time. Couldn't recommend more. If you don't know if you want this game or something else, pick this. it will take you beyond what you'd expect from an indie platformer, thats why I have 66 hours+ (also it's just THAT good)
This game truly tests your patience and your overall sanity.And I only speak for the main game, not the extra content.But even so, it's peak, I don't regret buying it.
I ♥♥♥♥ WITH THIS GAME HEAVY!! Played this before I realised i was trans and was goated since the start. Guess it's just in my blood 🏳️‍⚧️

This game cost me several burnout and several crashout
I breakup with my wife because of this
If you care for your mental health don't pay for this game

This game is awesome! It's one of the few games that just "clicked" for me - the lore, the music and the gameplay fit really well and the game, even though people say is "difficult", is really balanced and fair. For me it wasn't so difficult to 100% it, but still even now I'd come back to it and just enjoy the process of playing it. If you happen to struggle somewhere you can use "assist mode" to not be stuck at the same place - I personally didn't use it like at all so I don't know how much it helps. 
Buy the game: It's worth it!

[b]I don't play platformers.
I don't like really hard games.[/b]

Yet something about celeste just captured me in a way I never expected. I bought the game on a whim when I saw it was on sale and decided "Heck, why not?". I've heard of the game endlessly on different platforms with everyone talking about how amazing it was and how everyone should play it but I never really cared about that since I don't really play platformers or indies.

I will be trying more indie games from now on. That's for sure.

Where do we start?

[h3] GAMEPLAY: [/h3]
Its extremely simple, you have three buttons. That's it. Good luck. 
From those buttons comes a challenge that tests both your ability and your puzzle solving skills. There were several levels where it didn't really matter how flashy you were if you couldn't understand the basic room layout, and vice versa. Its managed to become one of those easy to play hard to master games(actually no its probably more like slightly difficult to play hard to master). Death means nothing more than another attempt at the same room, and you can genuinely see yourself getting better with each attempt. There was never a point in the main story where I thought it was "too hard" to do. Simultaneously there was never a point in the story where I found it too easy. They ramp up the difficulty over the course of the game, but they've done it perfectly to match your skillset.

[h3] STORY: [/h3]
Its a very simple premise that's presented beautifully. I did [i]not[/i] expect the story to be anywhere near this deep or this well made. The characters are loveable and each have their own unique personalities. Even in its short run time (you could probably finish the whole game within 10 hours) the game managed to make me care about these people and their individual journeys. I wont spoil anything else for you, but there's actually a lot more complexity to the story than you'd think. I feel it will be one of those tales that will stick with me for the foreseeable future.

[h3] GRAPHICS & ANIMATION: [/h3]
Every single level is beautiful. Sometimes I'd stop to just admire the detail on the blocks and the background. They have managed to make a pixel art game into one of the best looking games I have ever played. The animations are extremely smooth and fluid, the different elements have their own responsive movements and shape shifts to indicate what exactly is going on, which means you're never really stuck going "damn that didn't look like the thing I was expecting to happen".


All in all, this game has catapulted itself to one of my favorites of all time. Am I crazy enough to play the [spoiler] MUCH harder B and C versions of the levels that you get after beating the game? [/spoiler] Maybe, but I guess only time will tell. 

If you're wondering whether the game is worth your time, it absolutely is, and you absolutely should try it. Regardless of whether or not you're a platformer person or enjoy hard challenges. 

[b]I think Celeste is one of those games that everyone should play at least once in their lives.[/b]

Very great game. But at some points in it will be very challenging but so rewarding after managing a jump or a room. This game is not for people who get mad easily but it is also repetition if you die. So overall it is a pretty solid game.
This game is quite good tbh, really difficult at parts, the one bad feature is that English is represented with a Canadian flag. (obviously I'm joking)
peak platforming game. pun intended. great modding community too. gets very hard, but the effort is worth it :)
Amazing controls, amazing gameplay, memorable story, and beautiful art.
I love the story about Madeline fighting her demons and I also the difficulty. Has to be one of my all time favorite games!
Nice story with really great themes. Also had some really great gameplay, estrogen levels are up by 69% :3

Super good game, totally worth it if you like platforming. 

Some rooms however made me understand how Barbarousking felt playing Super sonic saves the world with how frustrating it can be to mess them up because of wrong imputs

The way this game makes me curse so much needs to be investigated (by a therapist idk). But I love it so damn much. I also like seeing the death count as the number of times I chose not to give up...which is in the thousands. But I didn't give up nevertheless.

[h1]The Strawberries represent Pain[/h1]

[h2]The Good[/h2]
[list]
    [*]One of the pinnacles of 2D platforming, this game offers nothing but that, just straight, raw, platforming mechanics, minimal combat, no skill trees, no equipment, full focus on challenging, smooth, and satisfying platforming
    [*]Platforming mechanics are introduced bit by bit, maintaining a very nice difficulty curve throughout the game, as you master one mechanic, to just be introduced to something new
    [*]The game offers more difficult content with the B and C Side levels, and final chapter, for players who crave more, but also an assist mode for those who need a hand
    [*]The game's narrative sends a meaningful message, which is simply weaved into the gameplay itself
[/list]

[h2]The Bad[/h2]
[list]
    [*]Narrative wise, dialogue is minimal and there isn't much to read about in terms of lore, so what you see is what you get (Doesn't mean the story isn't good, it's just mostly told via the gameplay and levels, and up to player's interpretation). Not really bad, just might not be everyone's preference
[/list]

[h2]The Random[/h2]
[list]
    [*]The speedruns of this game are an absolute JOY to watch, it's like watching the Matrix in realtime
    [*]Thank god Golden Strawberries don't have an achievement tied to them lol
    [*]This game strained my hands so much lmao
[/list]

[h2]Overall Thoughts[/h2]
Must try platformer for anyone who enjoys platformers, may be difficult for some player, but once mechanics click, and you triumph over certain sections, the satisfaction is like non other
I love games where depressed alcoholics struggle... oh wait I don't need a game for that
very fun but a bit hard so you might get better at platform games along the way
gave me inspiration for create my own game, the story is very interesting and character are just very cute. i really appreciate the art style...

Ive gotten up to summit so far and I am making my way past through the sludge section. After 4,900 deaths in the first 6 chapters (with chapter 3 and 6 having over 1,000 each) nothing really seems "hard" in the normal sense anymore. You just get into dash here jump here grab there mindset and the level just gets down to executing a string of movements. Its pretty poetic that the biggest takeaway from the game is patience and perseverence seeing as the game is about mental health. From chapter 1 to chapter 6, I have gotten much better at controls, such as being able to dash non-diagonally more consistently. I don't think I am ready to climb the summit yet, I think I will replay some of the past chapters before finishing it. 10/10 game, have 17 hours on my first playthrough save (just on the 6 chapters I have played)

This is my most played game on Nintendo Switch (my primary mode of gaming) so I decided to get it on Steam as an excuse to purchase it again. Though I vastly prefer the experience playing with controller, the patience and perseverance necessary for this game did change how I percieved my larger ability to work through challenges despite repeated failure. I only really played games like the sims before I tried Celeste so there was a STEEP learning curve. However after finishing it multiple times on Switch I’ve felt confident to try games like Hollow Knight and the Souls series that I was deeply interested in but saw as completely out of reach beforehand

Great game that can be enjoyed even if you aren't familiar with platform games, I've died like +3000 times but that's literally the point lol Trying until you get it right. I appreciate the help mode tho, there were levels (the last ones) that were really difficult for me so the help was very much appreciated lmao

Celeste is a fantastic game for beginner to intermediate players.  An inspirational emotional story between personas is likely the best! Climb, Cook and beat the times for speedrunning! Would highly recommend!

Pirated the game, beat it, bought the game, beat it again, i really like it.
really good music even to listen outside of the game and really good story.
gameplay is really satisfying but hard, if you find it too hard i recommend assist mode, i really struggled with B-sides the first time playing through them and slowed down the game for when i struggled too much for it to be worth for me

my brain is forcing me to play until I beat 1 or 2 chapter. Not gonna lie this game actually good and yeah honestly everything's good ok? my brain kept saying 'calm down and push it' with my burn eyes watching screen. I recommended it. thx for the promotion I can buy it with low price and this is the first game I ever pay.

There's a reason that "Difficult" is one of the tags for this game. I spent... just a little bit of time on the later parts of it. However, the amount of satisfaction when finally completing a portion that I'd been banging my head against for the last several days... well there's almost no better feeling.

This game is beautiful for his art style in every level, his characters, the ambient, the story and everything.
The game is quite challenging but its fun to play and for me it will be one of the best indies i ever play.
It was awesome
My top 3 game of all time.Must try game with  amazing mod support

this game made me see the world in a different perspective and i love that. every second i played this game was pure fun (and also a little bit a rage of course). to whoever wants to try celeste PLEASE DO!!!

First played this game on console, finished a lot, but then I wanted to look into mods, so I got it on pc. Beat it, got 6 goldens, did chapter 9, finished the c sides, and now I just got the moon berry, which I thought was not possible. Now I'm sitting on 100% and a second finish of one of the greatest games I've ever played. Everybody should buy this!!

This game is great, I'm addicted. The base game is very challenging for new players, but if you commit yourself to it you'll find progression comes easier than expected. The skill ceiling is limitless, and the modding community has thousands of maps that fit anyone's skill level, as well as maps that extend far beyond what the base game ever did. Honestly, the sheer content load of this game impresses me, and in my opinion, I think this is the highest quality precision platformer.

I had a blast playing this game. I have around 600 hours in Geometry Dash (I've completed several hard demons) so I thought this would be a breeze, but it was not. The game is genuinely challenging after chapter 2.

The soundtrack is actually what made me try out the game to begin with, and yeah, it did not disappoint. My favorite track "Resurrections" already plays in chapter 2 so that was nice.

Story wise it's great, I enjoyed all the characters and the evil clone thing was very cool. I died around 1500 times on my first playthrough to the summit. So I did struggle with some bits but overall it was moderately challenging. 

I like most of the mechanics, some of the obstacles feel a bit like BS (especially the Checking In chapter) but all the jumping and dashing feels smooth and satisfying.

can highly recommend getting this game if you want to listen to good music while platforming.

It is a really fun and challenging game that I have enjoyed quite a lot, and will enjoy for a while longer
9/10
This is one of my absolute favorite games of all time. After having played every level, I don't really know how else to describe it other than just... my favorite game.
Heard a lot about it before finally playing. Lives up to the reputation. Rewarding when you finally conquer the challenge

At the time of posting this review, I recognize that this game has long been elevated to the status of masterpiece since its release many years ago. If you've heard this and are looking through reviews because you're unsure if you should play it: You should. If you want something more detailed, great—because I simply must praise this game in more detail. This game's design should be studied. It is so wise in how it handles the interplay between gameplay and story and it is the epitome of nearly perfectly executing exceedingly simple concepts. The choice to segment the game into each of its nine chapters is already phenomenal, as each has its own goal and narrative focus (beyond merely finishing the chapter) that allows the player to easily compartmentalize and digest each piece of the overarching story. But then individually, each of those chapters sensibly establish and subvert design assumptions that mirror (pun intended) Madeline's internal struggle. As a consequence, the gameplay, beyond being designed well mechanically, produces feelings in the player similar to those experienced by Madeline by deliberately influencing game progression. In this way, the gameplay itself becomes a storytelling device. That is to say, for example, if a chapter feels confusing and directionless, Madeline likely feels similarly. It's an amazingly simple but unique way of allowing the player to realize and intertwine themselves with the story—without requiring them to exert any additional effort.

And that is all to say nothing of the artistry. A review won't do any of the art justice. Just experience it yourself. But I will say that while the pixel art nor the writing particularly blew me away, they're still really nice. Most of the emotional heavy lifting I think is done by the music. This game is sometimes jokingly referred to as the trans game for how many people resonate with the experience it depicts. Without the raw stimuli of the music, I'm skeptical if all the humanity that was baked into this game could've been realized at anywhere near the level of salience and profundity that it has been by so many people on such a large scale if Lena Raine did not make this soundtrack. She is a dangerously powerful artist, and this whole development team is dangerously creative. Celeste is rare relic capable of activating that humanity that unites all of us, serving as a profound reminder that the most powerful political rhetoric and deception will never be more powerful than the most powerful art.

I love this game so much, the fluidity of the movement and the storyline and the music and the gameplay are so so so good. Definitely give this game a try even if you aren't good at platformers because it is addictively good!
Ive played both, the pc, and console ports of this game and both times i had a blast beating the game. If you want a fun platformer with some story, then download celsete
Amazing Game. Love the platforming, love the visuals, love the music, love the story. I love every inch of this game, down to the last detail.
Very fun challenging game, unfortunately the game did not turn me trans (which may or may not be because I already came out as something else before playing it)

Yeah, you should buy it. It's a game with a good story and fun/hard challenges. The mechanics of this game are great too(ex:you sometimes have to use your velocity). Now yeah it is difficult, but i personally only needed 10 hrs to finish it(not 100% though) and you get used to the mechanics pretty easily. It also often goes on sale

10/10 
C7 last screen felt harder than farewell's last screen.
play only if you want bragging rights

scitzo girl face ghosts, demons, old ladies and ice walls like sum tomb raider type ♥♥♥♥
10/10 would recommend
Really good game, kinda short tho takes around 10 hour to beat but theres a lot of additional levels after you beat it. And sales can go up to 75% so if you buy it then its totally worth it

Did 100% in less than 80 hours.
One of, if not, the best platformer I have ever played. The story, the challenge, the gameplay fluidity, are all incredible... and the community? One of the most beautiful I have ever seen.
Totally recommend this game if you are into challenging-full-of-story games.

This, is trully the Elden Ring of platformers.
Great Platformer Good Achievements & Lots of post game challenges to do!

I started while using keyboard
Then I used a controller
It started glitching- dying constantly and when I plugged it in, dashing constantly
I got a nintendo controller connected
It started drifting
I still got the strawberry
10/10 game

So I just finished all of the game including B, C sides and Farewell (minus all the berries). Well, man this is one the best games I have ever played. I cant wait to explore the modded community and all it has to offer. The base story especially resonated with me as I have suffered from depression and anxiety in the past too. Would recommend 100% (even if it costed me a few keys on my keyboard at some points)
Even though I have like 2 hours I actually have 200 or so probably. I got it on itch and then modded it a bunch. Easily the best platformer of all time.

AWESOME! almost completed the base game after playing for 7 hours straight in 1 day... its good. its very good.
Every aspect of this game is exceptionally well-made. Dunno what more you could want.

I found it pretty difficult to finish. if you are not good at platforming, this is not the game for you. I do not regret playing it. The artstyle is beautiful and the story is enjoyable. I recommend supporting the developer, despite the levels being difficult.

Have you ever played Silksong and been like "Wow, I enjoyed the platforming. Wished it was just that and no combat" ? 
Me neither, but this game is solid, painful, challenging and almost always on discount. GG for those who try to get all the achievements.
Very good platformer with a touching message and good music. You will die a [b] lot [/b] but it's so quick that it never really becomes irritating.

Celeste is a fantastic game, easy to pick up, hard to master.
I'm trying to 100% it, maybe in another 80 hours!

Very difficult but fun 2D platformer.
Fun for mindless playing, unlike some metroidvania games.

I love this game. I just beaten all C-sides and I had soooo muchhh funnn. Every challenge, even if it took me hours didn't feel boring for a second. The movement is also AMAZING. For the first couple hours I really had to adjust my key binds a lot, but in the end I figured it out and it worked great on keyboard. The game is also just BEAUTIFUL! I love this game 100% recomend
Does what it says on the box, you climb a mountain. It earns its reputation, 10/10.
It is very challenging, even for completionists
amazing platformer, great wholesome chungus visuals and story
This game is extremely difficult and I hate it but I keep playing it so it must be good

'So fair and foul a game I have not seen!'
The main story on its own is rather difficult, and the B-sides and C-sides (as well as the final chapter after the main story, Farewell) are even more so hard. But it is extremely well-done; the learning curve is perfected so that you rather quickly become accustomed to the game's mechanics, the storyline is a wonderful touch, and the game's art and OST are masterpieces.
Buy it. You will hate yourself--but, still, buy it. It is *very* rewarding.

The story of this game is absolutely heart-warming. The difficulty is absolutely there, but that adds to the metaphor that the journey is always the most difficult part, therefore the part that you learn from the most. This game helped me through some difficult times, and though I've not completed the full main game (As in C-sides and goldens), speedrunning is really fun for any%! This one of my favourite games of all time, no doubt comparable with Hollow Knight, Silksong and games alike. I love the modding community, too, so thank you to Maddy and her team for creating this game in order for there to even be a modding community in the first place. Overall, this is higher than an A++ games and I love this game with all of my heart.

Для меня это игра-терапия. Каждый раз, когда плохо, эта игра меня спасала. 30 мая 2026 года я закрыл ее на все достижения. Это было долго, некоторые моменты (Особенно 7С и несколько экранов 9 главы) мне жестко надоедали, но я с ними справился. А если у меня хватило упорства справиться с этой игрой, значит хватит сил справитсья и с жизнью. Невероятное спасибо разработчикам за их великий труд, а тем, что читает данный отзыв, советую купить эту игру и пройти хотя бы основную кампанию. Не думаю, что вы пожалеете.

The pinnacle of 2D platforming and refined movement. 10/10

The game has good story, music, and art style. Everything complements each other nicely which creates a memorable experience. They serve their purposes well, but I wouldn't go as far as saying that they're masterpieces. On the other hand, the platforming is the greatest I have ever seen in any video game. It's a beautiful mix of simplicity and versatility. The player is only given the ability to jump, climb, and dash. And yet, the movement feels so clean and crisp. The game never feels unfair or unpredictable because every action is so straight-forward. Even more so, these simple movement options can be combined to create precise and complicated tech, further expanding the skill ceiling.

This game is a fun (but not easy) experience for casual players. And it becomes infinitely more fun for someone who's willing to put in the effort to become a skilled player.

I've never forgotten my first play through of this game on Xbox with 24 hours sunk in. Do not pass this up if you're looking for a heartwarming story and bang for your buck game.

I didn't really have high expectations when going into this game. Not for the *actual game*, I knew it would be amazing, but more for myself. I am NOT a veteran of the infamously difficult precision platformer genre, so going into this I was a bit worried my personal level of skill and experience would make this the classic case of "great game, but not for me." 

I absolutely don't blame anyone for having this experience with the game of course but oh my god. I think it was about halfway through Chapter 1 where the game really clicked with me. I don't know if it was the controls which feel *so* good to finally get the hang of, the wonderful and varied level designs, or the fact that the game is as forgiving as it is brutal. Really, I think it was all three.

Celeste is a game that wants you to succeed. So, so badly. It's a game that makes you feel proud of your death count. It's a game where you're consistently surpassing your own expectations for yourself (I was joking with friends that I probably wouldn't be able to get all the red strawberries... boy was I wrong). It's a game where completing that difficult room you've been struggling with feels like as much of an achievement as reaching the summit itself.

The game's story is simple yet perfect; "Climbing the mountain" is a vague enough goal that your interpretation of the MC's journey will always feel deeply personal, while still absolutely encouraging you to connect with and root for her as her own distinct character. Reaching the summit is something you want to achieve not only for yourself but for Madeline.

It's such a lovely game. I would highly recommend it if you're up for the challenge.

banger of a game, great gameplay great story and great soundtrack, the bsides are cancer inducing tho

This is definietly my favourite 2d platforming game. 
Some of my favourite things about Celeste:
*It has great music
*The platforming feels good. The game keeps introducing new stage elements, and sometimes even teaching you new things about things you have already seen. While it can be fast paced, most of the time trying to get through a screen feels more like a puzzle of figuring out the correct route/angle than a simple test of reaction time, which I liked a lot. I tend to have a hard time "seeing", when the screen is changing too fast.
*I really liked how the game handled difficulty. To start off, you can modify the game in a lot of ways to suit you, such as slowing everything down or getting infinite dashes. I frequently used this for backtracking when I wanted to reach a specific point quickly, and then I can simply turn it off again when I want to play normally. Every screen transition being a checkpoint makes it so you can usually get right back to where you were very quickly after dying. I even did a lot of the optional challenge stages, which I do not usually enjoy. Not all of them though (yet). 
*Rooms, which are both bigger than the screen and doesn't have places where you can stop and think, usually have binoculars that let you look ahead and get mentally prepared.
*I also quite enjoyed the story. I may have even cried a little.

Thing I dislike:
*There is a "postgame" stage which has a gate that can only be opened by having enough collectibles HALFWAY through the stage. I was really in the zone, both regarding the gameplay and the story, only to find out I needed to leave. This really brought me out of the mood for a while.
I was intentionally skipping all the B-side stages until I ran out of "normal stages", both because I was enjoying the story and because I knew they would be more difficult, so I expected I would need all the practice. There is, to my knowledge, no indication that you were intended to do this stage after doing the optional B-side stages. Simply putting the gate at the beginning, which is where it was in the previous chapter, would be much nicer. I started stage, saw that there wasn't a gate, and assumed that I would be able to proceed through the entire stage.
Dont like platformers, love this game and its story. Trans Rights! 8/10

Celeste is seriously one of the most beautiful games I've played, especially from the story's perspective. Despite it being hard as hell, Celeste teaches you that it's okay to ask for help, and that you shouldn't feel ashamed of needing assistance to enjoy the game. I relate to this a lot irl, and while I didn't need assist mode to fully complete Celeste, I really respect this game for its message and accessibility. This is one of the best platformers I've played, and it's one I want to come back to often.

Game Difficulty: Varies, but around 4-9/10
Game Enjoyability: 9/10
Love this game, the story, the mechanics, and its difficulty (and if it gets too hard, you can adjust with assist mode). This game is the perfect platformer.
